历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2022 to 2025, the minimum ranking for Physics Category (including Science) was approximately 5180 → 3433 → 3695 → 5133. After a significant advance in 2023, it has been declining in the past two years. The baseline for 2025 is about 5133. The provincial Physics Category enrollment plan is 40 students in 2025 and 38 in 2026, a slight reduction of 5%, leading to slightly intensified competition and a decreasing trend in ranking numbers.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 Project and Double First-Class university. On the Soft Science main ranking, it is approximately 73rd in 2026, with recent rankings fluctuating between 60 and 73, overall stable. China University of Petroleum (East China) has no significant negative or positive public opinion incidents in reputation documents; its reputation is stable. On Douyin heat data, the university did not enter the top 100, with average exposure. No record of enrollment gaps. Petroleum and Natural Gas Engineering, Geology, etc., highly correspond to the university's enrollment majors such as Petroleum Engineering and Resource Exploration Engineering. Majors like Petroleum Engineering and Oil & Gas Storage and Transportation Engineering have stable to slightly cold social heat. The total number of examinees in the province in 2026 is about 66,000 (uncertain), a significant increase from 44,491 in the Physics Category in 2025, intensifying competition.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The university is located in Qingdao, Shandong, and recruits across provinces in Ningxia, with no local enrollment preference. In 2025, the Physics Category plan of 40 students accounts for about 91% of the university's total plan of 44 in Ningxia, making it the absolute main category.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 5133, the slight reduction of 5% in the 2026 plan (ranking number decreases by about 154), the significant increase in the number of examinees in the province (intensified competition pushes ranking numbers up), and the stable university reputation, the reduction effect and the examinee increase effect offset each other. The ranking is expected to advance slightly compared to 2025. The predicted minimum ranking is about 5050, with a range of 4800–5300.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: For rankings within the predicted range of 4800–5300, it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors as the main direction, Tier 1 majors can be attempted (electrical, petroleum engineering and other flagship majors require higher rankings), and Tier 3 majors serve as safety options. For rankings significantly better than the lower limit of the range (e.g., within 4800), it is advisable to focus on Tier 1 flagship majors. Petroleum engineering, oil and gas storage and transportation engineering, and other petroleum-specific majors have high employment matching rates, suitable for examinees with clear industry intentions.
